questions.question: Self-attention allows each token to:
options: Only attend to the previous token
Attend to all other tokens in the sequence simultaneously
questions.explanation: Each query attends to all keys: Attention(Q,K,V) = softmax(QK^T/sqrt(d_k))V. Full sequence context in one operation.

questions.question: Multi-head attention runs multiple attention heads to:
options: Capture different types of relationships at different representation subspaces
questions.explanation: Each head can focus on different aspects (syntax, coreference, semantic). Outputs are concatenated and projected.

questions.question: Positional encoding in transformers provides:
options: Information about the position of each token — since attention is order-invariant
questions.explanation: Without positional encoding, the model treats 'cat sat mat' and 'mat sat cat' identically. PE adds position information.

questions.question: BERT uses which training objective?
options: Masked Language Model (MLM) — predict randomly masked tokens
questions.explanation: BERT masks 15% of tokens randomly and trains to predict them, learning bidirectional context.

questions.question: GPT's autoregressive generation works by:
options: Predicting one token at a time, each conditioned on all previous tokens
Sampling from the full vocabulary at once
questions.explanation: GPT: P(x_t | x_1,...,x_{t-1}). At inference, generate token, append, generate next — until EOS token.

questions.question: The Feed-Forward Network (FFN) in each transformer layer:
options: Applies the same two-layer MLP to each position independently
questions.explanation: FFN = Linear → ReLU → Linear. Same parameters applied to each token position — adds non-linearity after attention.

questions.question: Layer Normalisation in transformers is applied:
options: Before or after each sub-layer (pre-LN or post-LN) to stabilise training
questions.explanation: LayerNorm normalises across the feature dimension (not batch), stabilising deep transformer training.

questions.question: The T5 (Text-To-Text Transfer Transformer) model treats all NLP tasks as:
options: Text-to-text: input and output are always text strings
questions.explanation: T5: 'translate English to German: The cat sat' → 'Die Katze saß'. Unifies translation, QA, summarisation under one framework.

questions.question: KV-cache in transformer inference:
options: Caches key and value matrices from previous tokens to avoid recomputation
questions.explanation: During autoregressive generation, only the new token needs new Q. Old K,V are cached — avoiding O(n) recomputation.

questions.question: Flash Attention improves transformer training efficiency by:
options: Using tiling to compute attention in SRAM rather than HBM — reducing memory I/O
questions.explanation: FlashAttention reorganises the attention computation to minimise slow HBM reads/writes, achieving 2-4x speedup.
